displayName.description: CdoId: 1200; FieldId: 7992; Workspace: csi; Flags: ReadOnly; Annotations: DisplayName is the field generally used to represent an object in a user interface, especially when the user interface needs to represent an object with just one data element.  For example, to represent a revisioned object one would ordinarily need to show both the name and the revision of the object.   The display name will include enough necessary elements to represent the object--in the example of a revisioned object it will include both the name and the revision, with a separator character.   This is especially useful when viewing objects in combo boxes and other one-column lists, the display name should be used.  DisplayName is usually a non-persistent field.

durationPerUnit.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 7130; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This value specifies the amount of time needed to process a single unit. This is used in cycle time and Cost Accounting calculations.

fastCycleTime.description:
CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3968; Workspace: csi; Flags: ReadOnly; Annotations: This is a calculated value that denotes the time required processing a standard size batch of material using this Spec, assuming fast queue time. This is used to determine, for example, how quickly a job can be expedited through the Factory.

The calculation for this value uses attributes of other CDOs. This means that changing a Work Center value, for example, can effect this value. This means that this value should be calculated each time that it is needed. The value that is persisted will only be valid as of the point in time that this Spec was saved.

isFrozen.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 8324; Workspace: csi; Flags: ReadOnly; Annotations: Determines if changes are allowed to this instance (or any of its revisions)

normalCycleTime.description:
CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3969; Workspace: csi; Flags: ReadOnly; Annotations: This is a calculated value that denotes the time required processing a standard size batch of material using this Spec, assuming normal queue time.

The calculation for this value uses attributes of other CDOs. This means that changing a Work Center value, for example, can effect this value. This means that this value should be calculated each time that it is needed. The value that is persisted will only be valid as of the point in time that this Spec was saved.

runRateOption.description:
CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3971; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This option determines if run rate is specified as hours per unit or units per hour or time per batch. The values and their meanings are:
1 = Hours per unit (RunRateTime)
2 = Unites per hour (RunRateQuantity)                                                                                                                                                       
3 = Time Per Batch (RunRate Time)

setupTime.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3973; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This value specifies the time required for a Setup at this Operation. This is used in cycle time and Cost Accounting calculations.

stdBatchSize.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3974; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This value specifies the standard size of a batch of material processed using this Spec. This is used in cycle time and Cost Accounting calculations.

timePerBatch.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 27388; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This value specified the time needed to process a single Batch.

unitsPerHour.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3972; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This value specifies the number of units processed in an hour. This is used in cycle time and Cost Accounting calculations.

unscheduledTime.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 27387; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Process time that is not contained in the UPH,HPU or Batch Time entries.

yield.description: CdoId: 4670; FieldId: 3975; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Specifies the standard yield expected for processing with this Spec. This value is used in cycle time and Cost Accounting calculations.

notificationTargets.description: CdoId: 4730; FieldId: 4022; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: A Notification Target is used to define the recipient of a message that originates during the processing of a transaction. An e-mail message is a typical example. The Notification Target describes the mechanism for delivering the message (e-mail, page, fax, system alert, etc.) and any additional details that are required for a particular delivery mechanism.; OriginalType: NotificationTarget

An e-mail message is a typical example. The Notification Target describes the mechanism for delivering the message (e-mail, page, fax, system alert, etc.) and any additional details that are required for a particular delivery mechanism.; OriginalType: NotificationTarget

relevantServiceName.description: CdoId: 4730; FieldId: 4023; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: A service  to which this WIP message applies.  Note this list needs to be at the base service level. (i.e., the user specifies Move, not MyMove.  MyMove is always automatically included if move is specified). This field is not persistence and need to display on the screen.

relevantServiceType.description: CdoId: 4730; FieldId: 5364; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: RelevantServiceType. This field is not displayed on the screen

status.description:
CdoId: 4730; FieldId: 4013; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Determines the current status (Active, In-Active) of this instance. Valid values and their meanings are:

1 = Active
2 = Inactive

displayName.description: CdoId: 3730; FieldId: 7291; Workspace: csi; Flags: ReadOnly; Annotations: DisplayName is the field generally used to represent an object in a user interface, especially when the user interface needs to represent an object with just one data element.  For example, to represent a revisioned object one would ordinarily need to show both the name and the revision of the object.   The display name will include enough necessary elements to represent the object--in the example of a revisioned object it will include both the name and the revision, with a separator character.   This is especially useful when viewing objects in combo boxes and other one-column lists, the display name should be used.  DisplayName is usually a non-persistent field.

label.description: CdoId: 5130; FieldId: 4357; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: Identifier for relating a WIP Message to one or more Steps. WIP Messages defined for all Modeling Entities except a Step or Operation can be qualified with a WIP Message Label. This allows the same message to be associated with multiple Steps.

Bearer.description:
Authorization header using the Bearer scheme. 

 Enter 'Bearer' and then your token in the text input below.

Example: "Bearer 12345abcdef"
Bearer.name: Authorization
